title: Trump announces ceasefire with Iran and offers two-week window for diplomacy
subhed: The US president, who had vowed to annihilate Iran, announced a ceasefire with Tehran on Tuesday evening, which should lead to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Israel said it supported the agreement but insisted that its operations in Lebanon were unaffected.

title: French hostages return home after more than three years in Iranian prison
subhed: Cécile Kohler and Jacques Paris landed in Paris on Wednesday morning, following years of detention on espionage charges.

title: Stunning footage from NASA's Artemis II mission over the Moon
subhed: The crew passed within 6,545 kilometers of the lunar surface during the night of Monday, April 6. Their observations, lasting more than six hours, focused on 30 sites.

title: In catacombs, museums, and labs, the delicate heritage of human remains
subhed: The Paris Catacombs reopen to the public on Wednesday, April 8, after five months of renovations. The timing offers a chance to reflect on how human remains are handled in cultural and research institutions. These singular relics pose ethical, technical and legal questions.

title: 'The US administration's dysfunctions and the Iranian regime's resilience are driving Trump further into an obsession with force'
subhed: Five weeks into the US bombing campaign against Iran, Donald Trump's trademark unpredictability has been overtaken by the unpredictable dynamics of the war itself, observes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

Why is Donald Trump so mean?
subhed: By shamelessly flouting democratic norms and flaunting his amorality, brutality and bad faith, the American president restores pride to his humiliated electoral base, unsettles his opponents, and subverts the rules of the political game.

title: Viktor Medvedchuk, the Ukrainian traitor Putin wanted to install in place of Zelensky
subhed: Captured in April 2022 as he tried to flee Ukraine, the former MP and oligarch now lives in exile in Russia. As a lawyer in Kyiv in 1980, he sent his client, the great Ukrainian poet Vasyl Stus, to his death.

title: Sex is an art form hidden in the shadows
subhed: Moral panic over the pleasures of the flesh prevents us from recognizing the artistic dimension of sexuality, as well as from passing down the best practices, argues Le Monde columnist  Maïa Mazaurette.
